20-24: Sighted numerous bergs and growlers.
00-04: Ice observed: numerous large, medium and small bergs, bergy bits and growlers, no field ice.

0720: Commenced steering various courses skirting ice pack in Northerly direction. 0735: Stopped engines hove to and drifting. Sighted many bergs og all sizes. Reached edge of ice pack at 0720. Nine tenths coverage of broken block, brash, with no apparent leads.
08-12: Observed numerous bergs of all sizes, and ice pack with nine tenths coverage of broken, block, brash with one wide lead to NW.
12-16: Hove to at edge of ice pack; nine tenths coverage with broken block and brash.
16-20: Ice conditions, Mostly 5/10 coverage with many large and medium sized bergs, broken block and brash with occasional patches of clear water.
20-24: Encountered numerous bergs, growlers and broken field  ice during watch. Few leads and occasional patches clear water.
12-16: Ice: nine to ten tenths coverage with various leads; medium and small floes plus brash in the leads.
16-20: Ice condition; nine to ten tenths coverage of small and medium sized floes, 3-4ft thick and occasional pressure ridges, occasional leads.
20-24: Encountered numerous bergs, nine to ten tenths coverage of medium and large floes 3-4 ft thick with occasional leads.

00-04: Ice conditions; 9/10 concentration consisting of giant, medium and small floes with various leads and numerous puddles which are frozen, open leads consist of a thick concentration of small floes, brash and blocks; numerous large, medium and small bergs frozen fast in the ice.

04-08: Ice sighted nine tenths coverage 3 to 6 ft thick with occasional pressure ridges 2/10 brash and block and 7/10 large, medium and small floes, puddling accompenied by freezing of puddles lead 4/10 to 6/10 brash and block. Numerous large bergs frozen fast in the ice.
08-12: Observed bergs of various sizes, small, medium and large floes up to six feet thick, 8/10 concentration and various leads.

12-16: Continued from previous page. The number of large and medium bergs increased from many to numerous as ice floes lessened. 12-16: Ice concentration passed from 8/10 ro 2/10 coverage. Ice was made up of brash and water soaked floes with many leads and puddles.
16-20: Ice observed: small floes and growlers with small and medium sized bergs running in large lead of mostly open water, 2/10 coverage.

Note: Kap York brg 20 degs. True 3 miles at 1845.

00-04: Ice observed;Several large, medium and small bergs, numerous bergy bits and growlers.

12-16: Ice observed during watch; several medium bergs, widely scattered bergy bits, small floes and brash.

16-20: 2/10 coverage, mostly small floes with few medium size bergs, bergy bits and remnents of pressure ridges.
20-24: Observed 2/10 concentration composed of small floes, bergy bits and growlers, several large and medium bergs.

1140: Came alongside medium sized floe.

Ice_2: All size floes & small& meduim bergs

Ice_Log: 00-04: Gient and medium floes with occasional leads. Ice under pressure in spots with approx 9/10 coverage 16/20: Six to eight tenths coverage of floes and brash with few pressure ridges and numerous small bergs and bergy bits,  Floes and brash decreased at Cape Atholl water clear except for numerous growlers and bergy bits.

04-08: Ice 9/10 to 10/10 coverage Gient, large and medium floes, two to six ft thick and puddling Few leads in evidence. Occasional pressure ridges. Ice under moderate pressure at beginning of watch gradually easing to no pressure, with a few leads opening. Numerous bergs of all sizes.
08-12: Large and gient floes with considerable puddling; few bergs, few leads.

12-16: Many floes of old ice with some thick pressure ridges; numerous bergs and a moderate amount of growlers. At 1200 coverage was about 8/10 then reduced to 1/10 and by end of watch increased to 6/10
20-24: Mediou and small bergs, bergy bits and brasb, few growlers.

16/20: 5/10 coverage at beginning of watch small floes with much brash and blocks. Numerous growlers and medium bergs. Concentration increased to 8/10 mostly giant floes with some brash and a few medium bergs. At end of watch concentration decreased to 4/10, large floes and some brash, large areas of clear water with a few growlers and medium size bergs.
12-16: Small and medium floes with a few pressure ridges; numerous brash and growlers.

20-24: Large medium and small bergs, giant floes; 7/10 to 8/10 coverage consisting of remnants of pressure ridges, stringers, small broken up floes with large growlers, bergy bits, brash and rotten ice.
00-04: Many small and medium floes and several giant floes, coverage about 8/10 several pressure ridges, growlers and bergy bits and numerous large, medium and small bergs.

04-08: Three to four thenths coverage during greater part of watch mostly small and medium floes two to four feet thick and puddling. Occasional pressure ridges encountered on floes but ice deteriorating for the most part. Sighted few bergs due to poor visibility.
08-12: Some small and medium floes, few bergs and considerable scattered brash and bergy bits.
12-16: Scattered growlers and bergy bits with small bergs. At 1430 reached edge of ice; concentration 8/10, floes and bergy bits of old ice; remnents of pressure ridges.

16-20: Water mostly clear with patches of large and medium floes to 9/10 concentration. Clear water containing moderate coverage of small and medium bergs, many growlers and pressure ridges remaining after disintigration of floes. Ice generally deteriorating and well broken.
20-24: Water mostly clear with several bergy bits and small floes and remnants of pressure ridges; few large and medium bergs.

04-08: Ice concentration was about 6/10 at the beginning of the watch, consisting mostly of large and medium floes with much brask and block. Towards th eend of the watch the ship broke into a large lead, about 1/10 block and brash; Most of the ice was deteriorating and showed signs of having been under moderate pressure lately. Numerous bergs mostly large were sighted West of Kap York. Ice concentration seemed heaviest SW of Kap York.

0800: Numerous small and medium floes of rotten ice with occasional pressure ridges, numerous growlers and bergy bits, several small and medium bergs, Coverage about 6/10 08-12: Widely scattered bergs of all sizes, small floes and brash; numerous leads.

12-16: During first part of watch observed brash, blocks and medium floes; bergy bits and growlers.  Part of the harbor had 8/10 to 9/10 coverage and other parts 1/10 to 2/10 coverage except for that one belt one mile and a half wide from DeLong Pier.

Note: 1347: Moored along side DeLong Pier, Thule

Ice_Log: 16-20: Outer Thule harbor to Wolstenholme Island 1/10 to 8/10 with SE-NW bands of clear water, many bergs, bergy bits and brash.  Small floes one to four feet thick with some pressure ridge remnants of deteriorated floes.  Ice inside Wolstenholme Island generally small pressure ridge remnants and small floes 2/120 concentration.
20-24: Numerous bergs, small floes and pressure ridges.
04-08: Concentration 3/10 consisting of small floes and brash. Sighted numerous small bergs and growlers.
08-12: Observed small floes and brash, widely scattered, and several small bergs.
12-16: Observed small flows, numerous growlers and bergy bits, small and medium bergs; all well scattered.
16-20: Numerous bergs and growlers in 3/10 brash.
2211: Manouvering to skirt ice field.
2350: Observed edge of ice field: brash, broken block; a few small flows, remnants of pressure ridges and growlers, and five icebergs.

0230: Left edge of ice field. Ice observed brash anlong edge if ice field, ice field of 9/10 concentration mostly small rotten floe with many hummocks and few bergs. Left edge of ice field at 0230 and stood into open water with no ice in sight ahead.
04-08: Open water with few medium and large bergs.
08-12: Sighted a few large bergs. No sea ice.
12-16: Sighted a few small growlers and one small berg. Observed a moderate number of radar targets believed to be bergs and growlers.
16-20: Radar contact with several widley scattered bergs.
20-24: Observed large, medium and small floes of hummocky ice; many growlers and bergy bits whilst skirting th eice pack through brash.

00-04: Numerous large and medium bergs an dgrowlers and bergy bits.  Entered edge of ice field of giant floes 3 to 4 ft thick and concentration 10/10.  Drifted out of ice field into coverage of about 1/10.

Note: Position 13 miles  NNE True  of Cape Christian

Ice_Log: 04-08: Observed small floes and bergy bits in area of drifting.  Ice pack of 10/10 coverager was approx 1 mile dist, obscured after 0500 by fog.
0800 to 0930: Manouvering on various courses in leads running generally in a SW direction.

0930: Entered shore fast ice 10/10 concentration four to eight feet thick and puddling.  No leads in to beach in evicence.  Sighted a few bergs and picked up numerous large ice targets on radar.  Observed numerous pressure ridges running in a generally N-S direction.

Note: Cape Christian W Agnes Monument SE True

Ice_Log: 1200: Hove to as before in shore fast ice. 12-16: 10/10 coverage shore fast ice with puddling,  pressure ridges running generally N-S,  ice 2-6ft thick. Large clear water leads running south and containing scattered growlers and bergy bits in sight astern. .
1600-2400: Hove to in ice. 16-20: 10-10 coverage shore fast ice with puddles and pressure ridges,  2 to 6 ft thick.
20-24: 10/10 coverage,  shore fsat ice with puddles and pressure ridges,  two to six ft thick. 2000: Hove to in ice as before.
2200: Underway in ice pack.
2320: Negan breaking out shore fast ice on approach to landing beach.

00-04: large and medium floes, 2-5ft thick along with fresh brash concentration between 9/10 and  10/10 while hove to.  Numerous growlers and several bergy bits and large bergs,  a few medium and giant floes while underway in lead to open water. 0000: Breaking out approaches to Cape Vhristian near Agree Monument. 0019: Hove to in ice.
0110: Manouvering through the ice enroute Thule.
0243: Reached edge of shore ice and stood into open water.
04-08: Open water with few large and medium bergs, several bergy bits and growlers.  At 64-50W encountered edge of ice pack with several large floes extending from pack to westwards.
0630: Changed course to skirt edge of large ice floe extending from pack ice.
0742: Chnaged course skirting edge of ice pack.

08-12: Skirting western boundry of middle pack.  Concentration at pack limit appeared to be about 6/10 to 8/10.  Ran through several belts and tongues up tyo 4/10 concentration mostly brash and small floes.  Few bergs.
0901: C/C skirting ice pack.
12-16: Mostly scattered growlers and broken hummocks.  Passed through two lines of broken heavy floe ice running in a east x south direction,  Concentration about 3/10.
16-20: Radar contact with few widely scattered bergy bits.
20-24: Observed small bergs, bergy bits and growlers.  Passed a few toungs of brash and small floes extending westward from the middle pack.

00-04: Ice observed, none until 0100 then few growlers and hummocks coverage? then 1/10 gradually increasing to about 3/10 with small + medium floes, rotten old well ridged + numerous growlers and few bergs.
0112: Reduced speed to avoid growlers and small floes.
04-08: Sighted small medium and large floes up to 3ft thick,  numerous bergy bits and growlers.  Much puddling and many leads in evidence.
08-12: Bergs, large and small;  bergy bits, growlers and brash of 0 to 2/10, concentration.

12-16: At beginning of watch open water with some large bergs and 1/0 growlers and bergy bits.  Entered edge of ice field at 1400 - consisting of one to two tenths small broken floes with some remnants of pressure ridges.  Sighted a moderate number of medium and large bergs.
1351: Underway scouting best passage through ice.
16-20: 2/10 to 5/10 block, brash and glacial ice with moderate number of bergs and growlers.
20-24: Encountered about 2/10 brash and block and 3/10 glacial ice in Wolstenholme Fjord.
04-08: 4/10 coverage consisting of brask and block, small floes, numerous medium and small bergs, bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: 4/10 concentration of medium and small bergs, brash and block.
12-16: 4/10 all sizes burgy bits 1/10 brash.
16-20: Numerous bergs and bits of all sizes, 4/10 coverage in Wolstenholme Fjord.

20-24: Open water from Conical Rock to Kap York;  C onsiderable brash and occasional small puddling floes up to 2/10 concentration plus numerous burgs were encountered.  About 2300 ran into a field of giant and large floes 1-2 ft thick and puddling.  Concentration about 7/10 and generally rotten.

00-04: Small and medium floes of rotten, well puddled ice 1'-2' thick, brash, numerous bergy bits and bergs of all sizes.  Average coverage about 5/10, range from 2/10 to 8/10 until 0145 when open water was reached.
0145: Left ice field and stood into open water.

04-08: Observed no ice until 0550, passed 4/10 concentration of brash and blocks, small & medium floes, occasional bergs.  Concentration decreased to 2/10 untill 0700 when it increased to 4/10 with some remnants of pressure ridges.  Crossed belts of varying concentration reaching maximum of 8/10 at 0745;  Clear water observed (radar) ot port;  skirted edge of pack, observing 2/10 concentration at 0800.
08-10: Observed an average concentration of 2/120 composed of block, brash and growlers; few bergs.
12-16: Mostly open water with scattered bergs and growlers, occasional small areas of 2 to 3 tenths coverage of small broken floes and brash.
16-20: 1/10 to 5/10 broken remnants pressure ridges and brash; few bergs and growlers.
20-24: left ice pack at about 2015 and encountered no ice other than bergs and growlers for the remainder of the watch.
00-04: Many bergs of all sizes, few growlers.
04-08: No ice observed except for numerous bergs of all sizes, many bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: Observed numerous bergs of all sizes.  Last hour of watch the ice field apperaed 5 miles distant parallel to course line.

12-16: Three tenths coverage of medium and large floes one to four feet thick off Cape York with many leads.  Observed large shore lead extending to two miles off Capr York.  Five to eight tenths concentration of large floes one to four feet thick in Dead Mans Bay.  Numerous medium and large bergs and a few bergy bits. Floe ice well puddled and soft.
16-20: Observed few bergy bits and growlers;  south 2 miles, considerable field ice with bergs of all sizes.
20-24: Scattered bergs and bergy bits in wide shore lead off Cape York. Concentration of large bergs.
00-04: Few growlers, ship anchored in open water, two miles south many large and medium bergs in field of large floes.
04-08: Open water except for a few growlers an dfield ice and bergs 2 miles south.
08-12: Observed many large floes; bergs of all sizes and a few pressure ridges.

12-16: In vacinity of Conical Rock many small floes, growlers and burgy bits with many medium and large iceburgs.  Conical Rock to Wolstenholme Island one tenth or less composed mostly of growlers and burgy bits with occasional small floes.  At end of watch ice composed of growlers and burgy bits of all sizes with small amount of brash.

12-16: Less than 1/10 bergy bits and growlers.
16-24: Less than one tenth bergy bits and growlers.

04-08: Ice in harbor less than one tenth bergy bits.

20-24: Observed bergy bits and small growlers; 1/10 coverage.
00-08: Less than 1/10 coverager small bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: Many bergy bits and growlers less than 1/10 concentration.
16-20: Sighted numerous small bergs and bergy bits in Wolstenholme Fjord.
20-24: small growlers, numerous bergs of all sizes and numerous bergy bits.
00-04: Less than one tenth coverage consisting of many medium and large bergs, bergy bits and growlers. No field ice sighted.
04-08: From two to eight tenths concentration consisting of Giant and medium floes, hummocks and pressure ridges with several leads,  few bergs sighted, numerous bergy bits and growlers.
0723: Visibility reduced to less than one mile, encountered giant ice floes. ... various courses to follow leads through the ice.
08-12: Skirting large floes of heavy ice; met numerous brash and heavy block.  At 1000 to 1200 made little headway through giant floe of polar ice.
12-16: Vessel remained in lead of open water skirting heavy ice of 10/10 concentration.  Few puddles and some pressure ridges.

16-20: Encountered scattered small floes up to two tenths coverage while proceeding through open water from Foulke Fjord to Cape Dsabella.  Skirting large floes up to 10/10 with thickness up to six feet.
20-24: Skirted large floes up to 10/10 with 6' to 8' thickness, numerous growlers and bergs.
00-04: Open water except for scattered bergy bits and growlers.
04-08: Scattered large and medium bergs, bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: Few large and small bergs, bergy bits and growlers.
12-16: Open water except for a few small and medium bergy bits and growlers, occasional large bergs.
16-20: Sighted numerous bergs and growlers, no sea ice.
20-24: Observed numerous bergs of all sizes.
00-04: Numerous medium and large icebergs in vacinity.
Note: 0000: Underwat, drifting in Baffin Bay in vacinity of Kap Chalon.

Ice_Log: 04-08: Numerous large and medium bergs.
08-12: Observed several bergs of all sizes.
12-16: Open water except for a very few small and medium sized bergy bits and growlers.
16-20: Sighten numerous bergs and growlers, on sea ice.
20-24: Numerous bergs of all sizes.
00-04: Sighted a few large bergs, many small and medium bergs and a small number of bergy bits and growlers.
04-08: Observed few largeand many small and medium bergs; bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: Numerous bergy bits and growlers.

00-04: North Star Bight? one tenth coverage a? b?? of small tabular bergs, bergy bits and growlers.  Mid watc concentration devreased to numerous bergy bits and growlers and a moderate number of small and medium size bergs.
04-08: Numerous large and medium bergs, many bergy bits and growlers.
08-12: Observed scattered bergs of medium and small sizes;  bergy bits and brash of less than one tenth concentration.
12-16: Numerous small and medium floes, covering about 5/10,  Three to six feet in thickness with remains of pressure ridges.
16-20: Medium and small floes up to 8/10 coverage; considerable brash, some pressure ridges.
2105: Manouvering in ice.

2300: Sighted ice concentration of 6/10 to 8/10 of an ice field & giant, large, medium floes with numerous small bergs bergy bits & growlers with much brash & blocks - floes heavily pressure ridged and all weathered and from 4 to 8 ft thick.

00-04: From three tenths to eight tenths coverage.  In first half of watch mostly broken ice, remnants of pressure ridges generally from 4 to 8 ft thick  In latter half of watch mostly large and giant heavy floes with large pressure ridges with hummocks,  some broken pieces in leads between floes.  Sighted a few small and medium bergs. Ice increasing in coverage and thickness.
04-08: Six to seven thents concentration consisting of large, medium and small heavily hummocked floes.
08-12: Ice concentration of from 3/120 to 9/120 in heavily hummocked and well weathered ice field.  Lead has hummocky floes of all sizes and many small decayed growlers.

12-16: Numerous small and medium floes of well bummocked but rotting ice from 3 to 7 ft thick,  Also numerous growlers. Average coverage between 5/10 and 7/10 with several large bergs of 1/10 to 3/10 coverage.
16-20: Widely scattered brash and small bergy bits;  skirting edge of several giant floes of estimated 8-10 ft thickness and numerous pressure ridges.

20-24: Observed brash and block of 1/10 to 2/10 concentration in first two hours of the watch.  Observed large medium and small polar floes, six to ten ft thick;  concentration 10/10 during last hour of watch.

00: Hove to in ice in Lady Franklin Bay. 00-04: nine to ten tenths coverage with some very large hummocked polar ice floes.  small floes, blocks and some piling of loose ice in floes.  Occasional cracks and irregular small pools of clear water.
04-08: Nine to ten thenths coverage consisting of large and medium heavily hummocked polar ice floes, small floes and block, some puddling.

08-12: (Continued from previous page) No apparent open leads.  Ice shows signs of puddling but generally quite hard. 08-12: 9/10 to 10/10 coverage Giant floe at mouth of Lady Franklin Bay plus many large medium  and small polar floes heavily hummocked, 4 to 10 ft thick and under moderate pressure.
12-16: Numerous large medium and small floes of well hummocked polar ice from 4 to 12 ft thick with 10/10 coverage and occasionally puddled.
Note: Noon: Ladt Franklin Bay (Michael, just visiting the rellos) %^)

Ice_Log: 16-20: 9/10 to 10/10 coverage if ice 6-10 ft thick with many pressure ridges.  Some puddling and rotten ice, but generally hard and heavely hummocked.
20-24: Heavily hummocked and rafted ice of giant to medium flows with scattered puddling.  Concentration 9/10 to 10/10. Last part of watch worked the ship to a narrow blind lead.

00-04: Nine to ten tenths coverage of medium, large and giant floes with some block ice caused by pressure.  Floe ice well hummocked with occassional pressure ridges.  Shore lead of three tenths coverage, lead not continous.
0205: Broke out of ice pack at north of Lady Franklin Bay and entered narrow shore lead off Distant Cape.
04-08: Nine to ten tenths coverage consistion of medium, large and gianr floes, under pressure and heavily hummocked,  lead close to shore partially blocked by tongues of large floes.
08-12: Sighted ice concentrations of from 6/10 to 8/10.

12-16: Brash, growlers and small floes in open lead for about 1/10 coverage, then  numerous small and medium floes of well hummocked and slightly puddled polar ice from 5 to 15 ft thick and 9/10 to 10/10 coverage.

16-20: Heavy floes (of all sizes) of polar ice from 6-15 ft thick under varying degrees of pressure;  current appeared to be carrying off-shore ice in southwesterly direction.  Heavy hummocking abd some puddling in evidence.
20-24: Heavy hummocked and much rafted ice of 9/10 to 10/120 concentration.  Followed lead close to shore which proved to be blind.

00-04: General motion of large floe we are in to the eastward end? of Lincoln Bay with some filling in of loose ice into the Bay.  Rotation of floe counterclockwise. Floe ice eight to twelve ft thick, ten tenths coverage with a few small discontinous leads  becoming visible along the edge of the floe.

04-08: Nine to ten tenths concentration of giant and medium heavily hummocked floes under pressure.  With flooding of tide general motion of ice floes to south west;  Floe ice six to twelve ft thick, few leads not continous.
08-12: Ice concentration 8/10 to 10/10 heavily hummocked and rafted.  Many pressure ridges with few leads heavily concentrated with small floes, blocks and brash with some puddling.
12-16: (Continued) of well hummocked polar ice 8 to 12 ft thick with 10/10 coverage. 12-16: Ice observed, medium and large floes (to be continued next page).
1600-2400: Underway as before hove to in ice. 16-20: 9/10 to 10/10 coverage of giant floes, heavily hummocked, with some puddling.  One open lead to eastward gradually turning northeast.
20-24: Consolidated ice; large and giant floes of polar ice under pressure with few polynyas and leads; entire field drifting southwards at a fraction over one knot.

0000: Hove to in the ice in Robeson Channel off Cape Frederick VII. 00-04: Nine tenths coverage large and giant floes 7-15ft thick with Polynyas and few discontinous leads.  General ice drift to the southwest. No chnage in pressure on ice throughout the watch.
04-08: Nine to ten tenths coverage, giant and large floes, heavily hummocked; pressure easing with change in tide at 0400;  general drift of ice to southwest.
1040: Attempted to turn over screws but attempt was unsuccessful because of heavy ice pressure around screws, and side of ship.
1115: Sighted ice concentration 10/10. Ice heavily hummocked with much rafting because of heavy pressure, also prevalance of frozen puddling.
12-16: Observed medium and large floes of well hummocked and puddled polar ice of 5 to 15 ft thick for 10/10 coverage, puddles? have been freezing over. Ice is under pressure.
16-20: 10/10 concentration heavy polar ice.  Well hummocked but with some puddles of slush.  At one time during watch pressure appeared to ease, but insufficient to allow making way.
20-24: Observed heavy polar floes of 10/10 concentration under constant pressure.  Ice drifting southwestwards at one knot.

0000: Props stopped due to proximity of heavy ice under pressure. 00-04: ten tenths coverage mostly medium and large floes,  broken ice under pressure betwwen floes.  Floes mostly polar ice with hummocks.  General ice drift to the southward. 0000: Hove to in ice.
0200: Ice pressure easing slightly.
04-08: Nine to ten tenths coverage; large and medium floes, broken ice under pressure;  floes consisting of polar ice;  heavily hummocked, some floes of winter ice; general drift southwards.
08-12: 10/10 giant floes with hummocking, 6-15 ft thick under pressure.  Ship unable to make progress through ice.
1200: Hove to as before. 12-16: Medium and large floes of well hummocked polar ice 8-15 ft thick for a coverage of 10/10.  Ice is moderately puddled and puddles are freezing.
16-20: Ice as before with slight releif of pressure in evidence but no leads.   10/10 coverage heavy polar ice. .
20-24: Observed large, heavy floes of polar ice in a consolidated pack under pressure;  concentration 10/10.

0000: Hove to in the ice in Robeson Channel off Wrangel Bay. Heavy ice under pressure. 00-04: ten tenths coverage of large and giant floes with much rafting, many hummocks and occasional polynya.  Thickness fro 8-15ft & ice under moderate pressure.  Drifting slowly in a southerly direction.
04-08: Ten tenths concentration of large and giant floes, much rafting;  ice heavily hummocked, few polynyas; ice 8-15 ft thick under some pressure;  general drift of ice to south south west.
1115: sighten ice concentration 9/10 to 10/10 of heavily hummocked and rafted condition.  Polar ice a few polynyas and frozen puddles.
12-16: Small, medium and large floes of hummocked polar ice, from 5-12 ft in thickness.  Coverage between 9/10 and 10/10 with a shore lead visible towards Greenland.
16-20: Large and giant floes of softening polar ice, pressure easing.  Several leads of 2/10 to 3/10 coverage brash and small floes.
20-24: Passed from 8/10 coverage to 3/120 during watch, medium and small heavy floes;  block and brash with several leads.
00-04: Concentration one to five tenth in shore on eastern side of channel.  Ice in lead mostly small floes, blocks and brash.
04-08: One to three tenths coverage in open lead on eastern side of channel, small floes, blocks and brash.
1115: Sighted ice concentration of from 1/10 to 2/10 consisting of small and medium floes with mostly large blocks and brash.

12-16: Coverage 8/10 to 10/10 of well hummocked floes of all sizes, 5 to 15 ft thick.  At coast of Greenland brash, bergy bits, small floes and few small and medium bergs for a coverage of 4/10 to 7/10.
1300: Hove to at edge of ice field. 1455, Underway.
16-20: Rapidly increasing concentration of brash, growlers and small floes near Greenland shore.  Occasional belts of brash to westward, 2/10 coverage or less.
1700: Observed closing in of brash and small floes wwith steadily increasing concentration due to eastward drift.  1711 Commenced preperation for getting underway due to ice drift./.
20-24: Observed a few belts of brash when approaching the main pack; concentration less than 2/10.

0000: Hove to drifting in Kane Basin off Cape John Bonan? at edge of a concentrated ice pack. 00-04: Observed broken ice being driven off Elbourne Coast by northerly wind and moving toward pack.  Ice in open water broken floe ice with a few large hummocked remnants and occasional very small floe. Thickness 3-8ft.
0237: Hove to drifting. Drift during first half of watch SSW at 2 knots. Ice pack northern limit moving slowly south.
04-08: Ice conditions; one tenth concentration, small floes, block, brash and growlers, general drift of ice in SSW direction;  Drift of vessel SSW at 1.5 knots. 0400: Underway, drifting as before.
0800: Underway drifting as before.
0935: manouvering towards ice field.
1115: Sighted ice concentration 3/10 to 4/10 of large, medium, small hummockey floes with mostly blocks and brash - ice field several miles to the south.

12-16: Ice observed few small and medium floes, scattered ice, with block and brash allfloes hummocked and weathered.  Average coverage about 3/10 Ice fields of 8/10 to 10/10 coverage in sight to the north and south. 1246: manouvering clear of the ice.
1600: Underwat, drifting as before. 1603: Underway skirting ice pack. 16-20: Drift south at 1.2 knots.  Ice fields to north and south about 10 miles apart, 8/10 to 10/10 concentration.
2035: Commenced drifting. 20-24: Ice observed; few small and medium floes mixed with block and brash of a concentration of 3/10 to 5/10.
2350: Underway to avoid ice.

0000: Underway in Kane Basin manouvering clear of pack ice. 00-04: 0350 commenced drifting. Ice concentration from four tenths,  five to ten ft thick.  Northern edge of pack moving slightly south. Observed much brash and small floes drifting down from the north.  Drift through out watch when not underway SSW at 0.7 knots. .
0334: Underway at slow speed heading north away from edge of ice pack.

04-08: Standing away from ice pack.  Ice conditions; six tenths concentration consisting of small. large and giant floes,  flows hummocked with scattered glacial pieces, brash and block ice pack drifting south.  Drift of vessel when not underway SSW at 0.5knots
1145: Manouvering in ice.  Sighted ice concentration 5/10 of large medium and small floes with hummocks 5 to 8 ft thick.  some large blocks and many small block and brash.

12-16: Ice; few large and medium and small floe bergs, growlers and brash, numerous floes of all sizes.  Floes show signes of rotting and puddling.  Average concentration about 5/10 with many polynya and leads.
16-20: Ice observed; 8/10 to 9/10 concentration small medium and large floes 3-8 ft thick with hummocking. Leads short and discontinous.
2140: Commenced drifting. Ice; small large and medium floes 4 to 10 ft thick and some brash; concentration 8/10 to 9/10; few polynyas and blind leads.

0000: Drifting in large polynya in Kane Basin off Kap Russel. 00-04: Ice; drifting in a large polynya which gradually increased in size during watch.  Ice coverage eight to nine tenths, small medium and large floes with some broken brash between floes.  Many very narrow leads between floes. No appreciable drift during watch.
04-08: Ice; Seven to eigth tenths coverage, small medium and large floes many polynyas and broken leads, no appreciable drift during watch.
08-12: Sighted ice concentration 8/10 of large medium and small hummocky floes with frozen puddles - much block an dbrash, some frozen fast in sheltered areas.

12-16: Ice; numorous large and medium floes of rotting, puddling ice from 5 to 10 ft thick with on 8/10 coverage.  Few polynyas with block an dbrash; puddles and polynyas hav eformed young surface ice.
16-20: Ice; many large an dmedium floes of rotting ice, puddled, many polynyas and broken leads.
1801: Manouvering away from ice.
20-24: Ice observed; many large, medium and small floes of puddled ice and brash; many polynyas and broken leads.

00-04: Ice generally eitht tenths concentration with some polynyas.  Mostly small floes with nuch broken ice, occasional large and gient floes.  Ice well puddled and under no pressure. Four to tweltce ft thick.
04-08: Ice observed small medium an large floes of 3 - 8 ft thickness, 8/10 to 9/10 coverage, considerable puddling and hummocking; no pressure.
08-12: Sighted ice concentration 7/10 to 8/10 of moderatly hummocked floes of all sizes from 5 to 8 ft thick having numerous frozen puddles, many leads and polynyas mostly frozen 1-2 inches.

12-16: Numerous small and medium floes of rotting well puddled ice from 4 to 8 ft thick, also numerous block, brash and growlers and a few bergsfor average coverage of 8/10.  Many polynyas, all with grease ice about 1 inch thick.
16-20: Ice conditions four tenths decreasing to one tenth,  consisting of medium and small floes of rotting ice, well puddled, much grease ice approx one inch thick, brash, block and few bergs.
20-24: Scattered bergs, one tenth to two tenths consisting of rotting sea ice.
00-04: Entire watch in clear water with a few bergy bits and occasional small and  medium bergs. 00-08: Ice observed in North Star Bay; less than 1/10 coverage of bergy bits and growlers.
04-08: Open water except for widely scattered bergy bits and bergs of all sizes.
08-12: Several large, medium and small bergs and a few growlers.
12-16: Ice conditions unchanged. 12-16: Several large, medium and small burgs and several growlers.
Note: Noon: DeLong Pier, Thule. Noon: Smith Sound

Ice_Log: 16-20: Less than 1/10 concentration bergy bits and growlers in North Star Bay.
2358: Sighted small growlers and bergy bits.
00-04: Numerous bergs, bergy bits and growlers, all sizes, coverage less than 1/10, ship in open water.
04-08: Sighten numerous small and medium bergs and bergy bits, occasional growlers.  Many unsighted targets on radar, beleived to be burgs and bergy bits.
08-12: Ice observed; several growlers and bergy bits; many radar targets believed to be bergs and growlers.
12-16: Sighted several burgs and growlers and many radar targets believed to be bergs.
16-20: Sighted and had radar contact with many bergs and growlers at various times during the watch.
20-24: No ice sighted; several radar targets believed to be growlers and bergy bits.
04-08: No ice sighted, observed occasional small radar target beleived to be bergy bits and small or medium bergs.
08-12: Observed no ice other than a few radar targets believed to be small bergs.
12-16: Observed no ice several small radar targets believed to be bergs and a few burgs sighted..
16-20: Radar contact with few widely scattered bergs, bergy bits and growlers.

Note: Position 2 miles  E True  of Agnes Monument

04-08: Occasional large bergs observed visually in latter half of watch.
06-20: Sighted several medium and large bergs.
08-12: No ice sighted this watch.
12-16: Sighted a few large burgs this watch.
20-24: No ice
00-04: Observed two large iceburgs this watch.

12-16: No ice observed during watch.

20-24: No ice observed during watch.
